title: mfa — broadcast channels
description: MFA publishes NO broadcast channels. Real-time notification of MFA state changes to open browser sessions is not a use case in Wave 1a — a stolen session that had MFA disabled server-side simply fails on the next request (401), no push required. A future WebAuthn conditional-UI or mobile-push-based MFA flow would introduce a private channel here.
rationale: Real-time credential-registration confirmation (e.g. 'new hardware key registered on your account') is currently email-based via WebauthnCredentialRegisteredNotification. Push-based confirmation with a real-time reject option (`Approve this registration? Yes/No`) would need a private channel — deferred to Wave 2 alongside the mobile-push MFA feature.

Cross-tab session-revocation coordination on MFA disable (e.g. every tab logs out simultaneously when the caller disables MFA in one tab) is an `identity/auth` concern — the auth module owns Sanctum PAT lifecycle. When identity/auth ships broadcast-based session revocation, MFA-disable will piggyback on the same channel.
The step-up prompt in the SPA is initiated by the SERVER responding 403 MFA_CHALLENGE_TOO_STALE with an initiation URL — no push needed.
